channel.title: News on Computational Humanities Research
channel.description: Recent content in News on Computational Humanities Research

item.title: CHR 2025 in Luxembourg
item.description:
The 6th edition of the Computational Humanities Research (CHR) conference will take place at the Luxembourg Centre for Contemporary and Digital History (C²DH) at the University of Luxembourg from 9 to 12 December 2025.

Renowned for its focus on providing a dedicated venue for presenting computational work that bridges formal methods and traditional inquiry in the arts and humanities, the CHR conference offers a platform to explore computational, statistical, and mathematical approaches, alongside theoretical reflections on these methods. We look forward to continuing our discussions in Luxembourg!

item.title: CHR mailing list
item.description: On behalf of the CHR board, we are pleased to announce that we are setting up a mailing list for the CHR community. This will allow the board to share important updates with all members, and it will also enable community members to circulate relevant vacancies, calls, and other announcements.

item.title: CHR2027 in Manchester
item.description: We are pleased to announce that the 7th edition of the Computational Humanities Research (CHR) conference will take place at the Center for Digital Humanities, Cultures and Media at the University of Manchester from 5 to 8 January 2027. Find all information on the 2027 conference website.

item.title: New CHR board members
item.description:
​We are delighted to announce the appointment of two new members to the Computational Humanities Research (CHR) board: Lucy Li and Judith Brottrager.​

Lucy Li joins us as Proceedings Officer. She is a PhD student at the University of California Berkeley’s School of Information, focusing on natural language processing of social and cultural data.
